_meta.purpose: Visualization context for Qwen 7B — chart type selection, axis mapping, slicer layout, and dashboard design rules based on data returned from PostgreSQL SQL queries.
_meta.model_role: Qwen 7B receives: (1) the user question, (2) the metric identified, (3) the columns in the result data, (4) sample rows. It must decide: chart type, x-axis, y-axis, grouping, color palette, and slicer layout.

chart_selection_rules._note: NEVER use bar chart. Apply rules in order. First match wins.

rules.condition: Result has a Date or time column AND one numeric column
rules.reason: Trend over time — line is best for temporal data

rules.condition: Result has a Date or time column AND multiple numeric columns or a grouping column
rules.reason: Stacked area shows composition over time

rules.condition: Result has two categorical columns AND one numeric column
rules.reason: Heatmap shows two-dimensional categorical vs numeric
rules.example_question: Show bounces by branch and region

rules.condition: Result has ONE numeric column and user wants distribution/spread
rules.reason: KDE shows distribution shape of a numeric variable

rules.condition: Result has TWO numeric columns and user wants to see relationship
rules.reason: Scatter reveals correlation between two numeric values
rules.example_question: Is there a relationship between visit count and resolution?

rules.condition: Result has ONE categorical column AND one numeric column AND user wants spread/outliers
rules.reason: Boxplot shows distribution and outliers per category

rules.condition: Result has ONE categorical column with 3-8 values AND one numeric column (counts or percentages)
rules.reason: Single-row heatmap works well for bucket distributions

dashboard_layout_rules._note: All charts + slicers must fit on ONE screen — no scrolling for main view

layout.charts_area: Below slicers — 2 columns x 2 rows = 4 charts max on one screen
layout.chart_size: Each chart: approximately 50% width, 40% height of viewport

slicer_priority: Op bucket (always show if bucket data present)
TL or SH (if collection team question)

Bounce Count.title_template: Bounce Count by Branch and Region

Overdue Amount.title_template: Total Overdue by Region and Bucket

color_palette_guide.RdYlGn_r: Use for any metric where red=bad, green=good (bounce, NPA, flow)
color_palette_guide.Blues: Use for volume/count metrics (neutral blue scale)
color_palette_guide.Set2: Use for categorical groupings (buckets, products, teams)
color_palette_guide.Reds: Use for overdue/loss amounts — severity scale
color_palette_guide.tab10: Use when many categories need distinct colors (FE names, TL names)

labelling_rules.title_format: Descriptive + filters applied (e.g., 'Bounce Rate by Branch — Last 3 Months, VASTU Portfolio')
labelling_rules.legend: Always position outside chart to avoid overlap with data
labelling_rules.rotate_x_labels: Rotate 30-45 degrees if x-axis labels are branch names or dates

slicer_output_format._note: When Qwen 7B finalizes a dashboard config, it must return slicer definitions alongside chart configs

data_export.columns: All columns from the DAX result, plus applied slicer values as header metadata

chart_export.layout: Fit all charts to A4 landscape or A3 portrait
